quantification.why: Essentially every comparison carries a computed figure—product shares ($2,392.00, 33.4%), regional shares (30.4% vs 19.8%), and gaps ('roughly 10.6 percentage points behind North').

specificity.why: Each recommendation names a specific product/region with its figures ('Prioritize Gadget inventory... 33.4% of revenue from just 16 units', 'Increase West-region sales effort; West contributes only 19.8%', 'Review Sprocket pricing').

insight_density.why: Surfaces multiple non-obvious, actionable observations—Gadget's revenue concentration from only 16 units, West's ~10.6pp gap, and Sprocket's thin per-unit returns ('238 units sold yield only 15.8%').

quantification.why: Essentially every comparison carries a computed figure, e.g. Gadget at '$2,392.00 (33.4% of total) from only 16 units' and West 'roughly 10.6 percentage points behind North.'

specificity.why: Each recommendation names a product/region with data, e.g. 'Increase West-region sales effort; West contributes only 19.8% of revenue' and 'Review Sprocket pricing; 238 units sold yield only 15.8%.'

insight_density.why: It surfaces multiple non-obvious, actionable points like Gadget's high revenue from 'just 16 units sold' and Sprocket's thin per-unit returns from '238 units' at only 15.8%.

quantification.why: Essentially every comparison carries a computed figure—e.g., 'Gadget...at $2,392.00 (33.4% of total) from only 16 units' and West is 'roughly 10.6 percentage points behind North.'

specificity.why: Each recommendation names a specific product/region with figures, e.g., 'Prioritize Gadget inventory...33.4% of revenue from just 16 units' and 'Increase West-region sales effort; West contributes only 19.8%.'
